expectations.text: has_all_sections: Spec has all required sections including Acceptance Criteria
expectations.evidence: All 9 required headings present: ## Problem (L13), ## Solution (L17), ## Scope (L21), ## Acceptance Criteria (L40), ## Design Decisions (L51), ### Failure Modes (L65), ## Risks & Open Questions (L71), ## Alternatives Considered (L77), ## Verification (L83).

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Zero occurrences of ``` anywhere in the spec. No code fences in any section.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: No TypeScript types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L51-63) contains no 'interface ', 'type ', ': string', ': number', ': boolean', or '=> Promise'. All type descriptions are in natural prose.

expectations.text: no_json_shapes: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions describes the AlertTemplate message fields in prose (L53: 'An AlertTemplate message carries a display name, description, category tag...'). No JSON literals, proto definitions, or structured data shapes present.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope (L25-30) and Out of Scope (L34-38) contain zero file paths, no 'src/' references, no file extensions like '.ts' or '.tsx'. All items describe capabilities.

expectations.text: spec_under_120_lines: Under 120 lines excluding Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Implementation Delta begins at L115. Lines 1-114 = 114 lines, which is under the 120-line limit.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: 6/6 Design Decisions paragraphs open with bold topic sentences: L53 '**Templates are a distinct proto type...**', L55 '**The template variable system uses named placeholders...**', L57 '**The Alerts page entry point is a peer...**', L59 '**The dashboard context menu entry maps...**', L61 '**The template CRUD API follows...**', L63 '**Seed templates ship as part of...**'. 100% > 80%.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Implementer can start without questions
expectations.evidence: Spec names: proto type and CRUD API pattern (L53, L61), variable resolution strategy (L55), UI entry points and behavior (L57, L59), storage and bootstrap approach (L63), state coordination via created_from_template metadata (L53), and validation constraints (L55). Sufficient for a competent developer to begin.

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions (L51-69) contains zero code fences, zero TS interfaces, zero JSON shapes, zero exact line numbers, zero mechanism tables, zero React hook details. All decisions expressed as architectural prose with Always/Never/Ask First policy boundaries.

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: Failure modes are policy decisions
expectations.evidence: 3/3 failure modes are genuine policy decisions: (1) L67 structured per-variable validation errors vs generic 400 — a debatable API design choice; (2) L68 show message vs hide context menu item — explicitly notes the rejected alternative; (3) L69 idempotent bootstrap that preserves admin-modified templates — a deployment strategy decision. 100% > 50%.

expectations.text: scope_is_behavioral: Scope items are behavioral capabilities
expectations.evidence: In Scope items (L25-30): 6/6 describe behavioral capabilities — template storage with CRUD API, variable substitution system, template picker UI, dashboard context menu action, pre-built seed templates, template preview. Even L25 ('Server-side template storage with a new proto type and CRUD API') describes a capability, not a file path. 100% > 80%.

## Acceptance Criteria at L40 with 8 checkbox items (L42-49), within 4-8 range. All items are observable behavioral outcomes: button opens picker (L42), form presents variables (L43), submission creates AlertRule (L44), context menu shows action (L45), pre-fill mapping works (L46), preview renders (L47), API rejects invalid input (L48), seed templates available post-deploy (L49). Zero implementation tasks.

### Agent-Verifiable subsection at L99 with 7 checks (L101-107), all in action → outcome format: e.g., 'Send a ListTemplates API request → response contains the three seed templates' (L101), 'Send an InstantiateTemplate request with a missing required variable → response returns a structured validation error' (L103). Human-Only items (L109-113) are separate and clearly optional.

expectations.text: has_appetite: Spec has an Appetite field in the header
expectations.evidence: L5: '**Appetite:** Full Cycle (~6 weeks)' — present with a real time-budget value.

expectations.text: has_boundary_tiers: Design Decisions use three-tier boundaries where appropriate
expectations.evidence: All three tiers present across multiple Design Decisions paragraphs. Always: 'Always: store a created_from_template reference' (L53), 'Always: validate that all required variables are filled' (L55), 'Always: allow the user to override any pre-filled value' (L59), 'Always: make seed templates functional' (L63). Ask First: 'Ask First: if a variable type beyond string and numeric is needed' (L55), 'Ask First: if a panel's query uses features not representable' (L59). Never: 'Never: create a foreign-key dependency' (L53), 'Never: remove or gate the manual alert creation flow' (L57), 'Never: expose template deletion in v1' (L61). 9+ distinct tier statements across all 3 tiers.
